mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during low-inflation periods yield negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that consistently supports positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary conditions that compress future earnings multiples and generate negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and stagnant real personal consumption expenditures indicates inventory buildup that precedes corporate earnings revisions and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% while RSAFS monthly growth < 0.5%
failure_condition: The specified GDPC1 and RSAFS divergence pattern correlates with positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during high unemployment regimes reflects severe demand destruction that accelerates bearish equity positioning over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-unemployment periods produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed cases.
